headline: Forest City Reclamation Destroys 1,200 Hectares of Seabed in Johor
context: Forest City's Phase 3 reclamation in Johor destroyed an additional 1,200 hectares of seabed in 2024, with cumulative impact on fishing communities now affecting 4,500 households.

cards.big: Forest City has reclaimed 3,400 hectares of seabed since 2014.
cards.sub: 4,500 fishing households have lost access to traditional fishing grounds. Average catch is down 64%.

cards.big: The original EIA covered 1,386 hectares. Actual reclamation is now 3,400.
cards.sub: Subsequent phases were approved as 'amendments' to avoid full EIA reassessment.

cards.big: Forest City occupancy rate is 15% as of December 2025.
cards.sub: The environmental sacrifice has not produced the promised economic activity.

cards.big: Seagrass meadows supporting 47 dugong individuals have been fragmented.
cards.sub: Dugong sightings in the area fell from 12 in 2018 to 2 in 2024.

cards.big: 3,400 hectares destroyed. 15% occupied. The ecosystem loss is real; the development is largely empty.

Reclamation EIAs must cover the full planned extent, not phase-by-phase approvals that obscure cumulative impact. The Forest City approach — amendment by amendment — defeats the purpose of assessment.
